column2: La scala di rendering ti permette di usare una 'risoluzione inferiore' mantenendo tecnicamente la risoluzione nativa. This clause allows you to restrict the acceptance of a contract to only those that have a reputation equal to or greater than the defined amount.

There are two kinds of reputation you can specify here: 
 - {0}, which is the sum of all player's ratings, multiplied by a network effect.
 - {1}, which is the sum of the opinions from players you have rated favorably (and who they have rated favorably, etc).

You can review the reputations of all players on the server through the citizen list, which displays players in reputation order, and displays reviews from other players in the reputation tooltip.
column2:
Questa clausola ti consente di limitare l'accettazione di un contratto solo a coloro aventi una reputazione pari o superiore alla quantità definita.

Esistono due tipi di reputazione che puoi specificare qui: 
 - {0}, che è la somma di tutte le valutazioni del giocatore, moltiplicata per un effetto di rete.
 - {1}, che è la somma delle opinioni dai giocatori che hai votato in modo favorevole (e chi hanno votato favorevolmente, etc).

Could not create save file: {0}: Atmosphere pressure used in the atmosphere size formula. Trees absorb and pollution from tables produced in tons and converted to PPM by dividing on total atmosphere size. AtmosphereSizeInTons = WorldAreaInM2 * AtmospherePressureKgPerM2 where WorldAreaInM2 = (Dimensions.x * 10) * (Dimensions.y * 10). In example for 1km² (1000000m²) world with atmosphere pressure 1 kg/m² it will be 1000000 tons, resulting in 1 PPM per 1 ton of pollution or absorb (1 PPM =  1000000 * tons / AtmosphereSizeInTons), increasing pressure to 2 will result in 0.5 PPM per ton.
column2: La pressione atmosferica usata nella formula della dimensione atmosferica. Gli alberi assorbono e l'inquinamento prodotto dalle tabelle in tonnellate e convertito in PPM dividendo sulla dimensione atmosferica totale. AtmosphereSizeInTons = WorldAreaInM2 * AtmospherePressureKgPerM2 dove WorldAreaInM2 = (Dimensioni.x * 10) * (Dimensioni.y * 10). Ad esempio per un mondo di 1km² (1000000m²) con pressione atmosferica di 1 kg/m² saranno 1000000 tonnellate, risultando in 1 PPM per 1 tonnellata di inquinamento o assorbimento (1 PPM = 1000000 * T / AtmosphereSizeInTons), aumentare la pressione a 2 risulterà in 0.5 PPM per tonnellata.

Could not create save file: {0}: How old records can be grouped. It means it will try to process all last X days of records to group them. Usually you want to have this number low (1-2) in order to avoid process already processed records, but you can set high value (by example world age) in order to process at once all records from some old save with disabled grouping.
column2: Come sono raggruppabili i vecchi registri. Significa che proverà a elaborare tutti gli ultimi X giorni di registri per raggrupparli. Solitamente vuoi che questo numero sia basso (1-2) per evitare di elaborare registri già elaborati, ma puoi impostare un valore elevato (ad esempio l'età del mondo) per elaborare tutti i registri in una volta da qualche vecchio salvataggio con raggruppamento disabilitato.

Could not create save file: {0}: Max count of records that will be proccesed at once hourly. For reference, server with 100 players may reach 1.000.000 records per day. Usually 500.000 (default) should be ok, but you may want to tweak it if you have incredibly big count of records or if you are facing lag while it groups records.
column2: Il conteggio massimo di registri che saranno elaborati in una volta ogni ora. Per riferimento, i server con 100 giocatori potrebbero raggiungere 1.000.000 registri al giorno. Solitamente (default), dovrebbe andare bene, ma potresti volerlo modificare se hai un conteggio incredibilmente grande di registri o se riscontri lag durante il raggruppamento dei registri.

Could not create save file: {0}: This clause will grant the contractor authorization to the selected object or property. Authorization can be granted temporarily for the length of the contract, or it can be given permanently. This permission can be given at either the beginning or end of the contract.\n\nIf you need a player to deliver something in a private container, or build on private land, you'll need to set their permissions with this clause to ensure they have access. Players accepting a contract should always ensure that they will receive the needed permissions to perform it.
column2: Questa clausola garantirà al contraente l'autorizzazione all'oggetto o la proprietà selezionati. L'autorizzazione può essere concessa temporaneamente per la durata del contratto, o permanentemente. Questa autorizzazione può esser data all'inizio o al termine del contratto.\n\nSe necessiti che un giocatore consegni qualcosa in un contenitore privato, o di costruire un terreno privato, dovrai impostarne le autorizzazioni con questa clausola, per assicurarti che vi abbiano accesso. I giocatori che accettano un contratto dovrebbero sempre assicurarsi che riceveranno le autorizzazioni necessarie per eseguirlo.

Could not create save file: {0}: This clause allows players to define in words what they would like done. When using this clause, the contract can no longer be auto-approved, and the client will have to approve it before accepting it. When accepting a contract with a custom clause, it is wise to first review the reputation of the citizen offering it, because it is fully at their discretion to approve or deny its completion.\n\nYou must uncheck {0} at the bottom of the contract to use this clause.
column2: Questa clausola consente ai giocatori di definire a parole ciò che vorrebbero fosse fatto. Utilizzando questa clausola, il contratto non è più approvabile automaticamente, e il cliente dovrà approvarlo prima di accettarlo. Accettando un contratto con una clausola personalizzata, è saggio revisionare prima la reputazione del cittadino che lo offre, perché è pienamente a sua discrezione l'approvazione o rifiuto del suo completamento.\n\nDevi rimuovere la spunta {0} dal fondo del contratto per utilizzare questa clausola.

 - Removing unwanted plants

Could not create save file: {0}: This setting defines how plants will be grouped when the world generates. It have two levels of grouping: clusters and groups. A cluster is a large area of the world where a particular type of plant exists, and it affects gameplay by limiting where specific plants can be spawned. Groups are primarily for visual purposes, so plants of the same species grow together with only a small distance between them.
column2: Quest'impostazione definisce come saranno raggruppate le piante alla generazione del mondo. Dispone di due livelli di raggruppamento: cluster e gruppi. Un cluster è una grande area del mondo in cui esiste un tipo particolare di pianta, e influenza il gioco limitando dove possono generarsi delle piante specifiche. I gruppi hanno uno scopo principalmente visivo, quindi, le piante della stessa specie crescono insieme, con soltanto una piccola distanza tra di esse.

Could not create save file: {0}: A large bird with distinctive plumage and a fleshy wattle on its neck, which can change color based on its mood. Turkeys are highly social and known for their impressive vocalizations, including the famous gobble.

Could not create save file: {0}: A freshwater turtle with a distinctive hooked beak and powerful jaws, known for their aggressive behavior and ability to live in a wide range of aquatic habitats.

Could not create save file: {0}: A highly social rodent that lives in extensive underground burrows, with multiple entrances and tunnels. They use a distinctive bark-like call to communicate with other members of their colony, including to alert them of potential predators.

Could not create save file: {0}: A playful and agile semi-aquatic mammal, often seen floating on their backs or playing with objects in the water. Otters have a thick, waterproof fur coat that keeps them warm in cold water, and webbed feet that make them excellent swimmers.

Could not create save file: {0}: A wild goat species with shaggy white fur and curved horns, adapted to live in steep, rocky terrain. Their cloven hooves provide excellent traction and their white coat helps them blend in with their snowy surroundings.

Could not create save file: {0}: A large wild cat with distinctive black spots and a muscular build, often found in dense rainforests. Jaguars are apex predators and have no natural enemies, although they can be threatened by habitat loss and poaching.

Could not create save file: {0}: A fast and agile mammal, characterized by its long ears and powerful hind legs. Hares are generally larger and faster than rabbits, and they typically have a more solitary lifestyle.

Could not create save file: {0}: A small and agile canid with a reddish-orange fur coat and a bushy tail. Foxes are known for their intelligence and problem-solving skills, and have been observed using tools and displaying advanced problem-solving abilities in the wild.

Could not create save file: {0}: A majestic deer species found in forests and grasslands, characterized by its large antlers and brownish-gray coat. Male elk, also called bulls, grow and shed their antlers annually, which can reach up to 1.2 meters (4 feet) in length.

Could not create save file: {0}: A graceful, herbivorous mammal with a reddish-brown coat that inhabits forests and fields. Deer have evolved a variety of adaptations to avoid predators, such as their excellent sense of hearing, smell, and vision, as well as their ability to run fast and jump high.

Could not create save file: {0}: A crustacean with a hard exoskeleton and two large pincers used for defense, feeding, and courtship displays. Crabs are omnivores and feed on a variety of foods, including algae, other invertebrates, and carrion.

Annexing a target requires paying them Claim Papers from the source settlement.  Unlike resources, these 100% go to the foundation of the target without waste.  The cost is determined per-non-abandoned citizen in the target.\n\nIn the case of annexing a Homestead, this number will be increased by the number of Homestead Support Claim Papers that are on the stake.  This makes the support claims not only allow homesteads to claim more plots, but have more protection against annexation.

The resources shown must exist in storage attached to an Embassy Desk that is assigned to the Settlement's jurisdiction.  They must be accessible to the leader of the Settlement. The cost is determined per-non-abandoned citizen in the target.

Upon annexation, a percent of these will be given to the other party, and the rest destroyed as the cost of the annexation.

If all citizens of a target are 'Abandoned', no resources are needed.
